picks.rationale: JNJ shows stellar YTD +28.27%, 1M +17.83%, earnings beat with revenue up 9.9% YoY, a new 52-week high, and a dividend hike to $1.34 quarterly. [thestockobserver.com]

previous_picks.rationale: ABBV surged +4.01% today and is up +20.24% in the past month after its $10.9B Apogee acquisition and strong Phase 3 results; the stock added $17.8B in market cap in a single day and trades above analyst targets, with a P/E of 127.98 reflecting growth expectations. drivers: market cap surged $17.8B in one day

previous_picks.rationale: JNJ is up +28.27% YTD and +17.83% over the past month, with a low beta of 0.235 and a P/E of 30.48; the company's diversified healthcare model (pharma + medtech) and avoidance of GLP-1 hype appeal to risk-averse investors. Web search highlights its focus on oncology and medical devices, offering a cheaper valuation and higher dividend yield vs. pure-play pharma peers.

MRK is up +23.49% YTD and +13.77% in the past month, with a trailing P/E of 36.5 and a dividend yield of 2.62%; strong news sentiment (+0.299) and low volatility (beta 0.205) support its defensive growth profile.

GE is up +17.87% YTD and +19.98% over the past month, with a beta of 1.348 and a P/E of 46.96; strong analyst consensus (strong_buy, score 1.50) and positive news sentiment (+0.143) support the momentum in aerospace and energy transition themes.
RTX is up +15.47% over the past month and +4.18% today, with a P/E of 37.31 and a beta of 0.298; defense and aerospace demand supports stable earnings, while news sentiment is positive at +0.206.

V is up +15.92% in the past month and +9.56% over the past week, with a P/E of 31.54 and a strong buy analyst consensus (score 1.35); Piper Sandler initiated with Overweight on payments strength, underpinning momentum.

MA is up +14.39% over the past month and +10.32% over the past week, with a P/E of 31.25 and a strong_buy consensus (score 1.32); UBS highlights long-term growth after the Agent Pay launch, while the low beta (0.729) provides stability.
UNH is up +28.16% YTD and +13.47% over the past month, with a P/E of 32.05 and a low beta of 0.633; managed care tailwinds and earnings momentum support the stock, with a dividend yield of 2.18%.

BAC is up +12.66% over the past month and +6.11% YTD, with a P/E of 14.57 and a strong_buy analyst consensus (score 1.50); the low valuation and rising interest rate expectations benefit money-center banks.

NVO is up +20.07% over the past month and +5.86% over the past week, with a P/E of 12.09 and a dividend yield of 3.57%; UK Wegovy pill approval and expanded Medicare coverage are fresh catalysts supporting the GLP-1 franchise despite a YTD decline of -0.21%. Web search confirms the UK pill approval and Medicare access, though competition from Eli Lilly remains a risk.
